prompt: Photorealistic close-up product photography. A polished dark granite desk nameplate and small door plaque for a premium construction company. The logo on the nameplate is a dark charcoal pentagon shape — specifically a rectangle where only the top-right corner has been sliced off at a 45-degree diagonal, creating a distinctive five-sided shape (flat bottom, flat left side, flat top until three-quarters across, then a diagonal cut down to the right side, flat right side). Inside this dark pentagon shape, the letters LK are carved and filled with gold (#C9A96E) in an elegant serif font. A thin gold horizontal line sits near the bottom inside the shape. Below the pentagon shape, small refined capitals read DESIGN AND BUILD with generous letter-spacing. Also visible: a small A-frame easel-style sign in dark stained timber displaying the same pentagon logo mark. Sitting on a polished dark timber reception desk. Close-up shot on 50mm lens, warm interior lighting, premium construction office, Adelaide. Cinematic, elegant, photorealistic.
